originalTitle: At least 100 dead in Ebola outbreak in DR Congo, official says
title: Super Helpers Are Stopping a Sickness in DR Congo!
content:
Hello, Amazing Friends!

Hi everyone! It's your friendly African news reporter here. I'm so happy to share some important news with you today. We are going to talk about some very kind helpers. They are in a country called DR Congo, and they are working super hard. They want to keep everyone safe and healthy! It’s a big team effort, just like when you play a game with your friends. Everyone is helping each other to win against germs!

What is This Sneaky Sickness?

Sometimes, there are tiny, tiny things that we cannot see. We call these tiny things 'germs.' Some germs can make people feel sick. One of these sicknesses is called Ebola. Ebola is a bit of a sneaky sickness. It can make people feel very tired. It can also give them a fever, which means their body feels too hot. Sometimes, it can make their tummy hurt a lot. But here’s the good news! Ebola is not like a cold. You don't catch it just by being near someone. You need to be very, very close to a sick person's body to catch it. That's why doctors and nurses know just how to be safe. They are super careful! They have special ways to make sure they don't get sick while they help others. Did you know? The sickness called Ebola got its name from a river! It was first found near a river called the Ebola River. That’s how it got its special name, a long, long time ago.

Where is DR Congo?

DR Congo is a big, beautiful country right here in Africa. It is full of amazing animals, like gorillas and okapis. It has big, green forests and mighty rivers too. Many lovely people live there. Right now, some people in DR Congo are not feeling well. They are sick because of this sneaky Ebola sickness. Sadly, some people have become very sick and passed away. This makes many people sad. But please don't worry! Many smart and kind people are working very hard. They want to help everyone in DR Congo. Their big wish is for everyone to be healthy and happy again.

Our Heroes: Doctors and Nurses!

Many doctors, nurses, and health workers are in DR Congo right now. They are like superheroes, but without capes! They are helping the people who are sick every single day. They make sure the sick people get special care and medicine. They stay with them to help them feel better. It’s a very important job! These heroes wear special suits. These suits keep the germs away from them. It's like wearing a special shield all over their body! This helps them care for the sick people without getting sick themselves. Did you know? These special suits have gloves for their hands. They also have masks for their faces and special glasses for their eyes. They cover the helpers from head to toe to keep them super safe!

Magic Hands: Washing With Soap!

One of the very best ways to stop germs, including Ebola, is to wash your hands! You should wash your hands often. Use soap and water, and make sure to scrub, scrub, scrub! Try singing your favourite song while you wash your hands. This helps you wash them for a long, long time. It makes sure all the tiny germs wash away down the drain. Washing your hands often helps to keep those tiny germs away. It stops them from getting into your body. This is a super simple but very powerful thing we can all do to stay healthy!

The World's Super-Doctor Team: WHO

There is a very important group called the World Health Organization. We call them the WHO for short. The WHO is like a super-doctor team for the whole wide world! Their job is to help all countries stay healthy and safe. They know so much about germs and how to stop them. They help teach doctors everywhere. The WHO saw that many people were getting sick in DR Congo. So, they said it was a “big emergency!” This means they shouted: “We need to help RIGHT NOW!” They want to make sure everyone gets help very, very fast. Did you know? The WHO has experts from many different countries! They all work together to solve big health problems around the globe. They are like health detectives!

More Help Is On The Way!

When the WHO says it's an “emergency,” it means even more help is coming. More doctors and nurses from all over the world come to DR Congo. They bring special medicines and helpful tools. They also bring smart ideas to stop the sickness. Everyone works together like a big, kind team. They teach people how to stay safe at home and in their towns. They make sure sick people get the best care. And they try very hard to stop the sickness from spreading to more people. It’s a race against the germs, and our heroes are winning!

Special Shots to Make Bodies Strong

Sometimes, there are special “help shots” that can stop a sickness. These are called vaccines. Scientists have worked very, very hard to make these vaccines. They help people not get sick, or not get too sick, if they do catch the germ. The doctors are giving these special shots to people in DR Congo. Especially to those who are close to sick people. It's like giving their bodies a superhero shield against the germs! It helps them fight the sickness before it starts.

What Can YOU Do to Help?

Even though you are far away, you can still help in important ways! 1. **Wash your hands often!** Remember that magic trick with soap and water. Clean hands are happy hands! 2. **Tell a grown-up if you feel sick.** Always let an adult know if your tummy hurts, you feel hot, or just don't feel right. 3. **Learn about staying healthy.** Knowing about germs helps you stay strong and smart! 4. **Be kind and think about our friends in DR Congo.** Send them happy, hopeful thoughts! Being kind makes the world a better place.

A Bright Future Is Coming!

We know hearing about sickness can sound a bit scary. But please remember, many very smart and kind people are working super hard in DR Congo. They are using all their knowledge and strength to help everyone. They want to make sure everyone is safe and healthy very soon. With everyone working together, we can help our friends in DR Congo beat this sneaky sickness. We will make sure they are healthy and happy again. Let's send them all our positive energy and keep washing those hands! You are all amazing!
summary: Doctors and helpers are working very hard in DR Congo to stop a sickness called Ebola, with help from a big world team called the WHO, to keep everyone safe and healthy.
whyMatters: It matters because when people get sick, it makes everyone sad, so brave doctors and helpers are working together to make sure everyone in DR Congo can be healthy and play again.

originalTitle: What is Ebola and why is stopping this outbreak so difficult?
title: What is Ebola and why is stopping this outbreak so difficult?
content: An outbreak of Ebola in DR Congo involves a rare strain and is in an area affected by conflict.

originalTitle: Trump says he called off new Iran attack at request of Gulf states
title: Good News! Talking Instead of Fighting Far Away!
content:
Jambo, wonderful children of Africa! Your friendly news reporter is here with some very important news. It's about countries far, far away, but it's good news for everyone, everywhere! Are you ready? Sometimes, big countries have big disagreements. It can be like when you and your friend have a big quarrel, but much bigger! Today, we are talking about two countries called America and Iran.

Good News! No Fighting!

President Trump is the leader of America. He had a plan. This plan was about something called an 'attack' on Iran. An attack means fighting, like a big, big argument where things can get broken and people can get hurt. But guess what? President Trump said, 'No more attack!' He called it off! This means he stopped the plan. Hooray for stopping fights! Did you know? Calling off something means to cancel it. Like when your mom calls off your play date because it started raining!

Why Did He Stop It?

Why did President Trump stop the attack? Because other countries asked him to! These countries are neighbors to Iran. We call them 'Gulf states'. Imagine your neighbor asks you to please not fight with your friend because it makes everyone sad and scared. That's a bit like what happened here. These neighbors, the Gulf states, wanted peace. They told President Trump that talking is always better than fighting. Did you know? The 'Gulf states' are countries like Saudi Arabia and the United Arab Emirates. They are near a big body of water called the Persian Gulf!

What Are "Negotiations"?

So, instead of fighting, what will happen now? President Trump said that 'serious negotiations' are taking place. Negotiations! That's a big word, but it means something simple and good. It means people are sitting down and talking. They are trying to find a way to solve their problems without any fighting. Think about it like this: When you and your friend disagree about which game to play, you talk about it, right? Maybe you play one game first, then the other. That's a negotiation! Talking is a very strong tool. It helps us understand each other. It helps us find solutions where everyone can be happy, or at least mostly happy. Did you know? Talking things through is often called 'diplomacy'. It's how countries try to be friends and solve problems peacefully.

The Importance of Peace

Stopping a fight and starting to talk is wonderful news! Why? Because peace is so, so important. Peace means no fighting. Peace means people can go to school safely. Peace means families can be together without worry. When there is peace, children can play. When there is peace, farmers can grow food. When there is peace, everyone can live happily and safely. War, the opposite of peace, brings sadness. It can break homes and make people very, very sad. That's why we always hope for peace. Did you know? Many countries around the world have special groups of people whose job it is to help other countries talk and keep the peace!

Who is President Trump?

Let's talk a little more about President Trump. He is the leader of the United States of America. That's a very big country, far across the ocean. A president is chosen by the people of his country to lead them. It's a very important job, like being the head prefect of a very, very big school! Leaders like President Trump have to make big decisions. It's good that he listened to others and chose to talk instead of fight. Did you know? The United States of America has a special building called the White House where the president lives and works!

Where is Iran?

Iran is another country. It is also very far away from us in Africa. It is in a part of the world called the Middle East. Just like Africa has many different countries with different cultures and languages, so does the Middle East. It's important to remember that all countries are made up of people. People who want to live in peace, just like you and me. Did you know? Iran is a very old country with a rich history, beautiful buildings, and amazing stories! It used to be called Persia.

The Power of Talking

This whole story teaches us a big lesson. The lesson is that talking is always better than fighting. Even when things are very difficult. When you feel angry or upset, stopping to talk can make a big difference. It can change a bad situation into a good one. It's like when you're playing football, and someone fouls you. You could get angry and fight back, or you could talk to the referee or the other player and solve it. Choosing to talk instead of fight shows strength. It shows wisdom. And it brings hope for a better future for everyone. Did you know? Many wise elders in African communities teach children the importance of talking out problems to keep peace in the village!

Why This Good News Matters to Kids in Africa

You might be thinking, 'Why does this news from far away matter to me, a child in Africa?' Well, dear friends, the world is like a big, big family. When one part of the family is fighting, it can make everyone sad. And it can even affect everyone. When countries fight, it can make things difficult for trade. It can make things more expensive, even for the food and clothes we need here in Africa. But when there is peace, everyone benefits. Countries can work together. They can share ideas and help each other grow. So, news about peace, no matter where it happens in the world, is always good news for us too! It means a safer, happier world for all children, including you! Did you know? Africa is a continent with 54 amazing countries, each with its own unique stories and people, all hoping for peace!

A Happy Ending!

So, let's cheer for talking! Let's cheer for peace! It's wonderful that leaders chose to sit down and talk instead of fight. Remember this story when you have a disagreement with a friend or a sibling. Try talking first. It's the best way to solve problems and keep everyone happy. Keep shining brightly, children of Africa! Your friendly news reporter says 'Kwaheri' for now, and remember, peace is always the best way!
summary: President Trump of America decided to stop a plan to fight with another country, Iran, because neighboring countries asked him to talk instead, which means peace talks will happen now.
whyMatters: This is important because talking instead of fighting keeps everyone safer and happier around the world, making it a better place for children everywhere, even far away in Africa.

title: Man City's Shaw wins WSL Player of the Season
